violations.text: Food contact surfaces of a knife placed in the clean knife rack were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took knife and knife rack to 3-bay to be sanitized. Clean knife racks should only be used to store clean and sanitized knives to prevent contamination of other utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025
The chicken oven was observed leaking onto the floor in the kitchen area. Unit to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the sliding door tracks near the pizza display case and the fish display case have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Tracks to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025

The spray hose was hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. PIC removed hose. Work order was placed to repair broken hose rack. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025

The ceiling in the produce cut room had an accumulation of bio-film. The floors in various spots of the back storage area has a small accumulation of droppings. Areas to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025
A dead mouse was observed on a glue board in the deli area. Dead pests must be removed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

A gap was observed in the exit door near the entrance to the store. Door sweep/weather strip to be replaced/repaired. A gap was observed underneath the 5 door deli freezer. Hole to be filled/sealed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

A small amount of droppings were observed in various locations in the facility. Clean all droppings and report findings to pest control. The floors under the pet aisle must be cleaned due to various pet food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Various foods were observed held at 42-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was relocated to other refrigeration to cool down. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024
Baby formula was observed past the date on the can. PIC removed baby formula from shelf to receive credit. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The seafood display case was observed with an elevated temperature on the right side. Food was taken out of unit and relocated. Unit must not be used until serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The receptacle in the unisex/women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

The grout in the kitchen is worn down which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an small accumulation of rodent droppings/food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (automatic slicer, slicer, cheese grinder, cheese knives, and knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

Prepared chicken was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The following equipment was observed in poor repair: 1.Flatbread and sandwich cold hold unit was observed with ambient temperatures between 48-59 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC informed. Flatbread individual pizzas, which had just been prepared and placed in this unit, during the inspection were relocated to another cold hold unit. 2.gaskets and door curtains in the bakery walk in freezer. 3.door curtains in the seafood walk in cooler. Equipment components such as doors, hinges, seals, fasteners, and kick plates shall be intact ,tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturers specifications Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023
